verses.text: 'Oh that my head were waters... that I might weep day and night for the slain of... my people!' yet they bend their tongues for lies. The prophet weeps over them. A faithful heart mourns the very people whose sin it must rebuke.

'Oh that my head were waters... that I might weep day and night for the slain of... my people!' yet they bend their tongues for lies. The prophet weeps over them. A faithful heart mourns the very people whose sin it must rebuke.

'Oh that my head were waters... that I might weep day and night for the slain of... my people!' yet they bend their tongues for lies. The prophet weeps over them. A faithful heart mourns the very people whose sin it must rebuke.
Trust no brother or neighbour, for all deceive and 'through deceit they refuse to know me.' Society is rotten with falsehood. When deceit fills a people, even closest ties cannot be trusted.

Trust no brother or neighbour, for all deceive and 'through deceit they refuse to know me.' Society is rotten with falsehood. When deceit fills a people, even closest ties cannot be trusted.
God will melt and try them, for how else can He deal with such sin? 'Shall I not visit them for these things?' Refining and judgment must come. A people steeped in deceit cannot be left unpurged.
God will melt and try them, for how else can He deal with such sin? 'Shall I not visit them for these things?' Refining and judgment must come. A people steeped in deceit cannot be left unpurged.
God will melt and try them, for how else can He deal with such sin? 'Shall I not visit them for these things?' Refining and judgment must come. A people steeped in deceit cannot be left unpurged.
Jeremiah takes up weeping for the mountains and pastures, and God will make Jerusalem heaps, 'a den of dragons.' The land will be desolate. The beauty of the country becomes a haunt of ruin.
Jeremiah takes up weeping for the mountains and pastures, and God will make Jerusalem heaps, 'a den of dragons.' The land will be desolate. The beauty of the country becomes a haunt of ruin.
Who is wise enough to see why the land perishes? Because they forsook God's law, He will scatter them among the heathen. The cause is plain. Abandoning God's word brings exile and the sword.

He calls for the mourning women, for 'a voice of wailing is heard out of Zion,' How are we spoiled! Grief sweeps the city. The skilled mourners cannot keep pace with the coming sorrow.

Teach your daughters wailing, for 'death is come up into our windows,' and corpses fall like dung in the field. Death invades every home. No wall or window can shut out the judgment of God.

'Let not the wise man glory in his wisdom... but... that he understandeth and knoweth me.' True glory is in knowing God. Wisdom, might, and riches are nothing beside the knowledge of the LORD.
'Let not the wise man glory in his wisdom... but... that he understandeth and knoweth me.' True glory is in knowing God. Wisdom, might, and riches are nothing beside the knowledge of the LORD.

God will punish the circumcised with the uncircumcised, for 'all the house of Israel are uncircumcised in the heart.' Outward signs are not enough. God looks for the circumcised heart, not merely the body.
